adjudicator_mark.evidence: Second, it rules out the tempting fallback of renormalizing weights over the axes that did succeed: a composite without an accuracy score is meaningless under the gate semantics, because you cannot know whether the reply passed the gate at all. So the accuracy axis's role doesn't change *whether* a failed call must be excluded, but it does mean the record cannot be salvaged from the other two axes when accuracy is the one that failed.

votes.evidence: it does mean the record cannot be salvaged from the other two axes when accuracy is the one that failed... it rules out the tempting fallback of renormalizing weights over the axes that did succeed: a composite without an accuracy score is meaningless
The core principle — a failed call is missing data and must never be imputed as 0 — applies to all three axes equally, so in that sense the fix is axis-agnostic.

disputes.rule: two-of-three majority: the two primary judges' marks plus the adjudicator's mark; because the primaries split one-to-one on a disputed mark, the majority equals the adjudicator's mark, and the mark stays in every denominator
